triple_seq.solution:
Lemma triple_seq : forall t1 t2 H Q H1,
  triple t1 H (fun v => H1) ->
  triple t2 H1 Q ->
  triple (trm_seq t1 t2) H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M1 M2. intros h Hh.
  lets (h1&v1&R1&HQ1): M1 Hh.
  lets (h2&v2&R2&HQ2): M2 HQ1.
  exists h2 v2. splits.
  { applys eval_seq R1 R2. }
  { auto. }
Qed.
triple_seq.explanation: Sequence rule: evaluate t1 producing intermediate state satisfying H1, then evaluate t2 from H1 to Q.

triple_if_case.explanation: If-case rule: case split on the boolean, apply the corresponding branch triple.

triple_val_minimal.solution:
Lemma triple_val_minimal : forall v,
  triple (trm_val v) \[] (fun r => \[r = v]).
Proof using.
  intros. applys triple_val. xsimpl. auto.
Qed.
triple_val_minimal.explanation: Minimal spec for values: evaluates to itself with empty pre and equality postcondition.

triple_val'.explanation: General value rule: a value v satisfies any triple where H implies Q v.

triple_let_val.solution:
Lemma triple_let_val : forall x v1 t2 H Q,
  (forall v, v = v1 -> triple (subst x v t2) H Q) ->
  triple (trm_let x (trm_val v1) t2) H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M. applys triple_let.
  { applys triple_val. xsimpl. auto. }
  { intros v. xpull. intros ->. applys M. auto. }
Qed.
triple_let_val.explanation: Let-val rule: when the bound expression is a value v1, substitute directly and proceed.

triple_rand.explanation: Random number generation returns some m in [0,n). Use eval_rand to get the evaluation step.

triple_free'.solution:
Lemma triple_free' : forall p v,
  triple (val_free p)
    (p ~~> v)
    (fun _ => \[]).
Proof using.
  intros. intros h Hh.
  lets E: hsingle_inv Hh. subst.
  exists Fmap.empty val_unit. splits.
  { applys eval_free. }
  { hnf. auto. }
Qed.
triple_free'.explanation: Derive triple_free' directly from the evaluation rule for free.

triple_set.solution:
Lemma triple_set : forall w p v,
  triple (val_set p w)
    (p ~~> v)
    (fun _ => p ~~> w).
Proof using.
  intros. intros h Hh.
  lets E: hsingle_inv Hh. subst.
  exists (Fmap.single p w) val_unit. splits.
  { applys eval_set. }
  { hnf. auto. }
Qed.
triple_set.explanation: Set operation updates the cell at p from v to w.

triple_succ_using_incr.explanation: Allocate ref with n, increment it, read the result (n+1), free the ref, and return.

eval_like_eta_expansion.solution:
Lemma eval_like_eta_reduction : forall (t:trm) (x:var),
  trm_is_val t ->
  eval_like (trm_fun x (trm_app t x)) t.
Proof using.
  introv (v&->). intros s Q M.
  applys eval_fun. intros vf. applys M.
Qed.

Lemma eval_like_eta_expansion : forall (t:trm) (x:var),
  trm_is_val t ->
  eval_like t (trm_fun x (trm_app t x)).
Proof using.
  introv (v&->). intros s Q M.
  applys eval_fun. intros vf. applys M.
Qed.
eval_like_eta_expansion.explanation: Eta expansion: t and (fun x => t x) are evaluation-equivalent when t is a value.

triple_eval_like.solution:
Lemma triple_eval_like : forall t1 t2 H Q,
  eval_like t1 t2 ->
  triple t1 H Q ->
  triple t2 H Q.
Proof using.
  introv E M. intros h Hh. applys E. applys M Hh.
Qed.
triple_eval_like.explanation: If t1 and t2 are evaluation-equivalent, triples transfer between them.

eta_same_triples.solution:
Lemma eta_same_triples : forall (t:trm) (x:var) H Q,
  trm_is_val t ->
  triple t H Q <-> triple (trm_fun x (trm_app t x)) H Q.
Proof using.
  introv Hv. split.
  { intros M. applys triple_eval_like M. applys eval_like_eta_expansion Hv. }
  { intros M. applys triple_eval_like M. applys eval_like_eta_reduction Hv. }
Qed.
eta_same_triples.explanation: Eta expansion/reduction preserves triples for value terms.

triple_trm_seq_assoc.solution:
Lemma triple_trm_seq_assoc : forall t1 t2 t3 H Q,
  triple (trm_seq (trm_seq t1 t2) t3) H Q ->
  triple (trm_seq t1 (trm_seq t2 t3)) H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M. intros h Hh.
  lets (h'&v&R&HQ): M Hh.
  inverts R. inverts H0.
  exists h' v. splits.
  { applys eval_seq H5. applys eval_seq H6 H7. }
  { auto. }
Qed.
triple_trm_seq_assoc.explanation: Sequence is associative: (t1;t2);t3 evaluates the same as t1;(t2;t3).

triple_let_frame.solution:
Lemma triple_let_frame : forall x t1 t2 Q1 H H1 H2 Q,
  triple t1 H1 Q1 ->
  H ==> H1 \* H2 ->
  (forall v, triple (subst x v t2) (Q1 v \* H2) Q) ->
  triple (trm_let x t1 t2) H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M1 WH M2. applys triple_let.
  { applys triple_conseq_frame M1 WH. xsimpl. }
  { intros v. applys M2. }
Qed.
triple_let_frame.explanation: Let with frame: frame H2 around t1's triple, then use the framed postcondition in t2's triple.

triple_div_from_triple_div'.solution:
Lemma triple_div_from_triple_div' : forall n1 n2,
  n2 <> 0 ->
  triple (val_div n1 n2)
    \[]
    (fun r => \[r = val_int (Z.quot n1 n2)]).
Proof using.
  introv Hn2. applys triple_conseq triple_div'.
  { xsimpl. auto. }
  { xsimpl. }
Qed.
triple_div_from_triple_div'.explanation: Derive triple_div from triple_div' by extracting the n2<>0 precondition via consequence.

triple_factorec.solution:
Lemma triple_factorec : forall n,
  n >= 0 ->
  triple (factorec n)
    \[]
    (fun r => \[r = facto n]).
Proof using.
  introv Hn. gen Hn. induction_wf IH: (downto 0) n. intros.
  applys triple_app_fix. simpl.
  applys triple_if_case.
  { intros ->. applys triple_val. xsimpl.
    rewrite facto_init. math. }
  { intros ->. applys triple_let.
    { applys triple_add. }
    { intros r. simpl. applys triple_let.
      { applys IH. { math. } { math. } }
      { intros r'. simpl. applys triple_let.
        { applys triple_mul. }
        { intros r''. simpl. applys triple_val. xsimpl.
          rewrite facto_step. math. math. } } } }
Qed.
triple_factorec.explanation: Recursive factorial: base case returns 1, recursive case computes n * facto(n-1). Well-founded induction on n.
